mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances following a 0.5pp unemployment spike, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer and business demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, which improves short-term market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of periods following a positive INDPRO surprise, or the signal decays to zero predictive power within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above the 3.5% threshold forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or the inflation threshold loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer spending capacity, which directly boosts corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following positive PAYEMS revisions, or the payroll growth coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales validates robust aggregate demand across production and consumption channels, which elevates equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS signal fails to predict positive returns when both series grow above 2% quarterly, or the interaction effect is negative and significant (p < 0.05) in multivariate models.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply chain bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing activity, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances following a 1.0% INDPRO decline, or the signal shows no predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward testing.
